subject: Project Management
title: The Seven Wastes
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Seven Wastes.

hook.text: Ohno identified seven types of waste — 'muda' — that plague every process.

buildup.text: Overproduction, waiting, transport, overprocessing, inventory, motion, and defects. Seven deadly sins of waste.

discovery.text: Overproduction is the worst: making things nobody needs creates all the other wastes downstream.

twist.text: An eighth waste was added later: unused human potential — the waste of not listening to workers' ideas.

climax.text: Seeing waste everywhere is the lean mindset. Once you learn to look, you can't stop finding it.

punchline.text: Seven wastes. One simple question: does this add value?

quiz.question: Which waste did Ohno consider the worst?
options: Overproduction — it creates all other wastes downstream
Defects — they are the most expensive
Waiting — it wastes the most time
